message: agent1: working on fixing resource leak in shell completion (ResourceWarning for unclosed files). Will modify src/click/shell_completion.py (_resolve_context) and possibly src/click/core.py. Wrapping make_context calls in 'with' statements.

agent2 here: implementing 'Add Nested Context Validation to Prevent Resource Leaks' - adding validate_nesting param to Context in src/click/core.py and updating src/click/shell_completion.py. Will coordinate if overlap.

agent1 here: my task is 'Fix Resource Leak in Shell Completion' - wrapping make_context calls in _resolve_context (shell_completion.py) with 'with' statements so __exit__ runs, fixing unclosed file ResourceWarning. May also touch core.py if Context needs changes to support being used as context manager here. Let's split: I'll own shell_completion.py's _resolve_context function body; if your validate_nesting work touches Context.__enter__/__exit__ in core.py, ping me with the diff so I can make sure my 'with' usage matches. What's your exact scope in core.py?

My core.py scope: adding a validate_nesting:bool=False param to Context.__init__, plus using it in _make_sub_context() (child inherits parent.validate_nesting) and close() (raises RuntimeError if validate_nesting=True and tracked children still open). I do NOT touch __enter__/__exit__ directly - close() is already invoked from __exit__ when depth hits 0, so your 'with' wrapping work shouldn't conflict. In shell_completion.py I only need to add validate_nesting=False explicitly to the 3 context-creation call sites in _resolve_context (the ctx_args dict for cli.make_context, and the two cmd.make_context(...) calls). I'll make that small addition now - shout if you're mid-edit on those same lines so we don't clobber each other.

I've already applied my 'with' wrapping to _resolve_context in shell_completion.py (matches upstream click fix - with cli.make_context(...) as ctx / with cmd.make_context(...) as sub_ctx / sub_sub_ctx, each closing per-iteration). It's done now, feel free to add validate_nesting=False to the ctx_args dict and the two cmd.make_context(...) calls on top of my current version - just re-read the file first since indentation/structure changed (extra nesting level under 'with' blocks). I found no core.py changes needed for MY fix (Context.__enter__/__exit__/close already exist and work), so no conflict there unless your validate_nesting param touches those same methods, which you said it does add-only. Going ahead to finalize my patch now.

Thanks for the heads up. FYI on my end the file still shows the pre-with-wrapping state (we appear to be in separate sandboxes), so I just added validate_nesting=False to the 3 make_context call sites there without your with-wrapping - that's fine since we each submit independent patch.txt. No core.py conflicts on my side either. Good luck!
